Hey guys and welcome to your advanced visualization supplement. The first one we're really going to get started on here is the moment of highest tension. We're starting here because it's really the crux of everything we're doing here. It's own section later in the 15 point sales page in a day theme plate. At the end of the day, it's what motivates people to part ways with their money to invest in a new reality to empower themselves to take action on something. It is to solve the moment of highest tension, not some vague problem, not some condition which is placeholder for how it shows up, but this, the moment of highest tension. This is the thing that in their mind is what they are most afraid of, what they've experienced that they don't want to experience ever again. You present it in front of them in a very clear, compelling, specific way that is what's going to motivate them and empower them to take action. Just to be clear, the moment of highest tension is a hyper specific moment. It's something you could actually film. It's a real thing. If there were actors playing, you could give them the script describing the moment of highest tension and they would know exactly what to do. It is that hyper specific moment when that problem, which your product solves, flares up in the most intense way, in a way that your prospect can no longer ignore it. Let's say you are a tax accountant. The moment of highest tension would be the moment a business owner receives that letter from the IRS. They hear it in the mail slot. They touch it. They see those red letters on it that say urgent IRS or whatever and they know that it can't be good. They feel that visceral clench in their body because they know that something really bad just happened. See how many senses got engaged in that one picture. You could bring in sight, taste, sound, even that inner dialogue of what's happening in their head, them saying, I wish I just didn't put this off until now. You could reflect those aspects of it too, not only the outer experience in a specific way, but the inner experience of how that's happening, the clench in their stomach, the sweaty palms, all those things that really make up the scene where that problem presents itself in the most intense way where they'd be motivated to solve it. That's the moment of highest tension.
